items.title: Aerial photographer's hunt for 'Easter eggs' yields stunning images

children.content: Scott McCook spends his spare time photographing the salt lakes of WA's Wheatbelt, which look uncannily like giant eggs when viewed from the air.

gallery.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.
Composite: A pink lake surrounded by crop land. A red red lake framed by trees and pale farm land.

thumbnail.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.
Composite: A pink lake surrounded by crop land. A red red lake framed by trees and pale farm land.

items.title: Police launch full-scale search to locate woman missing in popular tourism spot

children.content: A large-scale search is underway for 51-year-old Naomi Marie Duckett, who was last seen at Gap Natural Bridge within Torndirrup National Park just before midday on Thursday.

image.altText: A head shot of a 51-year-old woman with black top and grey eyes.

items.title: World-famous Bibbulmun Track considered for heritage list

children.content: Councils support moves for the Bibbulmun Track to be declared a heritage-listed site, which conservationists hope could protect the track from future mining expansions.

image.altText: Hikers and karri trees on the Bibbulmun Track

items.title: WA's record for heaviest pumpkin broken

children.content: A pumpkin grown in Albany has beaten the previous WA record for heaviest pumpkin by nearly 60 kilograms.

gallery.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck
A forklift picking up a pumpkin
A medium shot of two sheep eating pumpkin

thumbnail.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck
A forklift picking up a pumpkin
A medium shot of two sheep eating pumpkin

items.title: Cyclone Narelle's destructive path across Australia, in pictures and video

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

gallery.altText: A man looks downcast as he looks at crops
Palm trees in the foreground with a red sky behind.
People look out at the coast as clouds gather.

thumbnail.altText: A man looks downcast as he looks at crops
Palm trees in the foreground with a red sky behind.
People look out at the coast as clouds gather.

items.title: Woman charged with murder following investigation into death of 24yo

children.content: The woman, 48, and the deceased were known to each other, according to Homicide Squad detectives.

image.altText: A shot of a grey building with the words 'central law courts' written on the side.

items.title: Queen at 100 is honoured in fashion exhibition as Andrew scandal bites

children.content: Within palace walls the Queen was a godlike figure. As her glorious fashion goes on display to commemorate what would have been her 100th birthday, how would she have responded to the Andrew crisis if alive today?

image.altText: Queen Elizabeth II in a long formal blue gown with bolero jacket and headpiece sitting on a red velvet seat with gold trim

items.title: Eagles back Reid after standing up for teammate in heavy loss to Swans

children.content: Eagles coach Andrew McQualter leaps to Harley Reid's defence after his discipline came into question during the 128-point thrashing dished out by the Swans.

image.altText: Harley Reid is involved in an altercation with Justin McInerney during the Eagles vs Swans AFL match in Perth.

items.title: Dirt bike rider, 14, among three dead in separate WA Easter long weekend crashes

children.content: The long weekend is off to a tragic start after two motorcyclists die in separate crashes near Perth, while a woman, 18, is killed in a four-wheel drive rollover in WA's north.

image.altText: A door of a police car with police tape tied to the side mirror.

items.title: Hospitality operators call for end to Rottnest's staffing advantage

children.content: Visa rules allow backpackers to conduct "remote work" on the island, just 33km from Perth's city centre, but not in WA's South West — something business owners want to change.

gallery.altText: Two girls at a coffee shop

thumbnail.altText: Two girls at a coffee shop

items.title: Does a 'historic' plan to protect WA's largest river go far enough?

children.content: A long-awaited draft plan aimed at protecting one of Western Australia's largest rivers has been released for public consultation, but traditional owners, environmental groups and pastoralists say key changes are needed.

image.altText: A river with treed land on either side.

items.title: 'Could get worse': WA Easter holiday-makers anxious amid fuel crisis

children.content: Accommodation providers in regional WA tourism hotspots see a high number of cancellations, as holiday-makers choose to stay close to home due to fuel prices.

image.altText: Large waves crash onto a rocky headland.

items.title: Why local fish may be hard to find this Easter in WA

children.content: This Easter is the first in Western Australia under tough new demersal fishing rules. Seafood retailers say the change in supply and price is noticeable.

image.altText: a person purchasing fish from a van

items.title: Who is eligible for the new nasal spray flu vaccine?

children.content: It's been available in the northern hemisphere for years, now Australian children can receive a nasal spray vaccine for the flu.

image.altText: A small child receives a nasal spray while sitting on a parent's lap

items.title: 'Adrenaline rush' of adaptive skateboarding redefines inclusive sport

children.content: Using a specially designed skate frame, adaptive skateboarding allows participants to stand safely while trained instructors guide and stabilise them.

image.altText: two people skateboarding using a harness.

children.content: The loopholes to look for if you're thinking about downgrading or changing your private health insurance.

items.title: More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV. Here's what to know

children.content: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering purchasing an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

image.altText: Image shows a close-up view of a man's hand holding an electric vehicle charger and inserting it in the charging point.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of Top Gun, bowling underarm and your weight on the Moon

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: Goose and Maverick from the film Top Gun

items.title: How do you clean a toilet brush and when should you change it?

children.content: If you're just shoving the brush into its holder to wait for the next use and actively avoiding thinking about how dirty the whole concept of it is, you're not alone. Here's how to make sure your toilet brush is as hygienic as possible while it lasts.

items.title: Police plunge into Perth's Swan River to rescue woman

children.content: Two police officers who swam out into Perth's Swan River early on Thursday to help rescue a woman will be recommended for a bravery award.

image.altText: A woman clings to a buoy in the Swan River as a police officer swims up to her.

items.title: Easter temperatures to drop in Perth as another Queensland cyclone looms

children.content: For most of the country, skies have cleared up just in time for Easter — but Western Australia perhaps has the most interesting weather ahead.

image.altText: A map of Australia with expected rain totals for the Easter long weekend, showing orange along the southern and eastern coasts.

items.title: Your pocket guide to daylight saving time ending in Australia

children.content: The air is getting cooler and the days are about to get shorter as daylight savings comes to an end this weekend.

image.altText: A woman reaches out to an alarm clock from bed.

items.title: Gordi helped write a song for her partner, then 'stole it' back

children.content: Gordi's new single was originally meant to be for her partner. Thankfully, both artists know the importance of trusting their gut.

image.altText: Gordi wearing a shiny jacket and holding the collar while standing against a dark background with pink lighting.

items.title: From farm to fashion festival: Indigenous designs stun on the runway

children.content: When a farmer and a First Nations artist join forces, something beautiful emerges — thanks to Japanese knitting technology.
